message:
Harden session lifecycle: reject TTL-less extend and fix completion semantics

Two distinct session management fixes:

1. extend_session() now rejects attempts to extend sessions that have no TTL
   set, returning an explicit error instead of silently computing a new expiry
   from a None base. Added test_extend_without_ttl_fails to verify.

2. ExecutionPlan::pending_actions() and is_complete() now treat
   IdentityMismatch entries (PID reused by a different process) as terminal
   alongside Applied and Skipped. Previously, identity-mismatched actions
   remained in the pending set, preventing session completion even though
   no further action could be taken. Renamed internal helpers from
   applied_set to completed_set to reflect the broader semantics. Added
   test_execution_plan_terminal_skip_not_pending to verify. Also updated
   ExecutionStatus::Skipped doc comment to clarify it means "process gone"
   rather than "already applied in prior run".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
